info.title: DeployHQ API Interactive Documentation
info.description:
# DeployHQ API

DeployHQ offers an API to allow interacting with our platform. You can find in this documentation a list of all the endpoints available and how to use them.

Please note we also offer a more user-centric API documentation available at [https://www.deployhq.com/support/api](https://www.deployhq.com/support/api).

## Access & Authentication

Users are provided with an API key which can be found from the "Security" page within the "Settings" menu. It's a 40 character string which must be used with your username in order to authenticate. All API requests should be sent with HTTP Basic Authentication with your username (email address) and API key.

All requests should also send the following headers:

- `Accept: application/json`
- `Content-Type: application/json`

These requests should be in JSON and all responses received will be returned as JSON.

Requests should be made to `https://<account>.deployhq.com/` replacing `<account>` with the name of your account.

account.description: Your account's permalink (e.g., sg, dhq, etc.)

get.description: Returns all network agents registered to the current account.

post.description: Claims an unclaimed network agent using its claim code and registers it to the current account.

put.description: Updates the name of an existing network agent.

patch.description: Updates the name of an existing network agent.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a network agent from the account.

post.description: Revokes the agent's claim, releasing it from the account so it can be reclaimed.
Registers a new DeployHQ account. No authentication required. Returns API credentials and SSH public key for immediate use. Rate-limited to 3 signups per IP per hour.

get.description: Returns all account-level environment variables available to every project.

post.description: Creates a new account-level environment variable. Locked variables cannot be read back after creation.

get.description: Returns the details of a single global environment variable.

put.description: Updates an existing global environment variable's name, value, or locked status.

patch.description: Updates an existing global environment variable's name, value, or locked status.

delete.description: Permanently removes a global environment variable from the account.

post.summary: Copy a global server to a project

get.description: Returns all projects accessible to the authenticated user, sorted alphabetically. Each project includes its starred status.

post.description: Creates a new project in the account. Optionally applies a template to pre-populate servers, config files, and other settings.

get.description: Returns the full details of a single project including its repository info, zone, and starred status.

put.description: Updates the project's settings such as name, notification preferences, zone, and permalink.

patch.description: Updates the project's settings such as name, notification preferences, zone, and permalink.

delete.description: Queues the project for deletion. Fails if any deployments are currently running.

post.description: Generates an AI-powered summary of changes between two revisions based on commit messages. Requires a configured and cloned repository.

get.description: Returns aggregated deployment statistics and per-server metrics for the specified time period.

post.description: Toggles the starred status of a project for the current user. Starred projects appear prioritized in listings.

patch.description: Uploads a custom SSH private key for the project and returns the corresponding public key.

get.description: Returns the webhook URL and auto-deploy settings for each server and server group in the project.

post.description: Enables or disables automatic deployments for individual servers and server groups. Unrecognized identifiers are skipped and reported in the response.

get.description: Returns all build cache files configured for the project.

post.description: Creates a new build cache file entry for the project. Build cache files persist between builds to speed up subsequent build runs.

put.description: Updates the path of an existing build cache file entry.

patch.description: Updates the path of an existing build cache file entry.

delete.description: Removes a build cache file entry from the project. The cached files will no longer persist between builds.

get.description: Returns all build commands configured for this project, ordered ascending. Build commands run during the build pipeline before deployment.

post.description: Adds a new build command to the project's build pipeline. Commands execute in order during the build phase.

put.description: Updates an existing build command's properties such as the command string, description, or error handling behavior.

patch.description: Updates an existing build command's properties such as the command string, description, or error handling behavior.

delete.description: Removes a build command from the project's build pipeline.

get.description: Returns details of a specific build environment configuration, including known hosts, cache files, and available packages.
Returns all build environment configurations for the project, including the default and any overrides.

post.description: Creates a new non-default build environment configuration that can override the default settings for specific servers.

get.description: Returns details of a specific build environment configuration, including known hosts, cache files, and available packages.

put.description: Updates a non-default build environment configuration, including its target server assignments.

patch.description: Updates a non-default build environment configuration, including its target server assignments.

delete.description: Removes a non-default build environment configuration. The default configuration cannot be deleted.

put.description: Sets the version of a specific language or runtime package for the project's build environment. Optionally targets a specific build configuration override.

patch.description: Sets the version of a specific language or runtime package for the project's build environment. Optionally targets a specific build configuration override.

get.description: Returns all SSH known host entries configured for the project's build environment.

post.description: Adds an SSH known host entry so the build server can connect to the specified hostname without host key verification prompts.

delete.description: Removes an SSH known host entry from the project's build environment.

put.description: Sets the version of a specific language or runtime package for the project's build environment. Optionally targets a specific build configuration override.

patch.description: Sets the version of a specific language or runtime package for the project's build environment. Optionally targets a specific build configuration override.

get.description: Returns all SSH commands configured for the project, ordered ascending. SSH commands run on target servers before or after deployment.

post.description: Creates a new SSH command for the project. Commands can be configured to run before or after deployment on specified servers.

get.description: Returns details of a specific SSH command, including its timing, target servers, and error handling settings.

put.description: Updates an existing SSH command's properties. Server assignments are only modified when server parameters are explicitly provided.

patch.description: Updates an existing SSH command's properties. Server assignments are only modified when server parameters are explicitly provided.

delete.description: Removes an SSH command from the project.

get.description: Returns all configuration files for the project, ordered ascending. Config files are deployed to servers with their content during each deployment.

post.description: Creates a new configuration file for the project. The file will be deployed to the specified path on target servers during each deployment.

get.description: Returns details of a specific configuration file, including its content body and server assignments.

put.description: Updates an existing configuration file's path, content, server assignments, or other properties.

patch.description: Updates an existing configuration file's path, content, server assignments, or other properties.

delete.description: Removes a configuration file from the project. The file will no longer be deployed to servers.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of deployments for the project, excluding previews. Can be filtered by target server/group or limited to currently running deployments.

post.summary: Queue, preview or schedule a deployment
post.description: Creates a new deployment for the project. Set mode to 'queue' to execute immediately, or 'preview' to generate a preview of changes without deploying. If a schedule block is provided, the deployment is scheduled for future or recurring execution. Use parent_identifier to target a specific server or server group.

get.summary: List log entries for a deployment step
get.description: Returns log entries for a specific deployment step, with optional pagination and download support.
Returns the full details of a deployment including its steps and log entries. Archived and legacy deployments return simplified responses.

post.description: Immediately aborts a deployment that is currently in progress.
Retries a failed deployment by resetting it and re-queuing. Only deployments that have completed (successfully or with failure) can be retried.
Creates a new deployment that reverts the project to the state of the specified deployment. Archived deployments cannot be rolled back.

get.description: Returns all environment variables for the project, ordered by name. Variables can be scoped to the build pipeline or specific servers.

post.description: Creates a new environment variable for the project. Variables can be locked to prevent value visibility and scoped to the build pipeline.

get.description: Returns details of a specific environment variable. Locked variables will have their values masked.

put.description: Updates an existing environment variable's name, value, or settings. Server assignments are only modified when server parameters are explicitly provided.

patch.description: Updates an existing environment variable's name, value, or settings. Server assignments are only modified when server parameters are explicitly provided.

delete.description: Permanently removes an environment variable from the project.

get.description: Returns all files excluded from deployment for this project.

post.description: Adds a file path to the project's exclusion list so it is skipped during deployments.

get.description: Returns the details of a single excluded file by its identifier.

put.description: Updates the path of an existing excluded file entry.

patch.description: Updates the path of an existing excluded file entry.

delete.description: Removes a file path from the project's exclusion list.

get.description: Returns all permanent integrations configured for this project.

post.description: Creates a new integration for the project. Only certain hook types are allowed via the API.

get.description: Returns the details of a single integration by its identifier.

put.description: Updates an existing integration's settings. The hook_type cannot be changed via the API.

patch.description: Updates an existing integration's settings. The hook_type cannot be changed via the API.

delete.description: Permanently removes an integration from the project.

get.description: Returns the repository configuration for the specified project.

post.description: Replaces the project's repository configuration. Any existing repository is destroyed and a new one is created with the provided settings.

put.description: Updates the repository's branch, root path, or hosting service type without replacing the entire repository configuration.

patch.description: Updates the repository's branch, root path, or hosting service type without replacing the entire repository configuration.

get.description: Returns a map of branch names and their latest commit references for the project's repository.
Returns detailed information about a specific commit including author, message, tags, and avatar URL. Queues a repository update if the commit is not found.
Returns the latest remote commit reference for the specified branch.

Returns recent commits and tags for the specified branch. Set update to '1' to fetch the latest data from the remote before returning results.
Renders the data for the commit selector popup
Returns all forthcoming scheduled deployments for the project, ordered ascending by scheduled time.
Returns the details of a single scheduled deployment.

delete.description: Cancels and deletes a scheduled deployment that has not yet run.

get.description: Returns all server groups for the project, ordered ascending.

post.description: Creates a new server group for the project to organize servers and control deployment order.

get.description: Returns the details of a single server group.

put.description: Updates an existing server group's settings such as name, branch, or transfer order.

patch.description: Updates an existing server group's settings such as name, branch, or transfer order.

delete.description: Permanently removes a server group from the project.

get.description: Returns all servers belonging to the specified project, including their agent associations.

post.description: Creates a new server in the project. The protocol_type determines which additional connection parameters are required: SSH needs hostname and username, FTP needs hostname, username, and password, S3 needs bucket_name, access_key_id, and secret_access_key.

get.description: Returns the full details of a single server including its connection configuration.

put.description: Updates an existing server's configuration. If the server uses external authentication and is not yet authenticated, a redirect to the OAuth URL is returned.

patch.description: Updates an existing server's configuration. If the server uses external authentication and is not yet authenticated, a redirect to the OAuth URL is returned.

delete.description: Removes a server from the project. Fails if any deployments are currently running against this server.

post.description: Clears the stored SSH host key for the server, allowing it to accept a new key on the next connection.

get.description: Returns all SSH commands configured for the project, ordered ascending. SSH commands run on target servers before or after deployment.

post.description: Creates a new SSH command for the project. Commands can be configured to run before or after deployment on specified servers.

get.description: Returns details of a specific SSH command, including its timing, target servers, and error handling settings.

put.description: Updates an existing SSH command's properties. Server assignments are only modified when server parameters are explicitly provided.

patch.description: Updates an existing SSH command's properties. Server assignments are only modified when server parameters are explicitly provided.

delete.description: Removes an SSH command from the project.

get.description: Returns all private templates belonging to the current account.

post.description: Creates a new template, optionally copying configuration from an existing project.

get.description: Returns publicly available templates, optionally filtered by framework type.

put.description: Updates an existing template's name or description.

patch.description: Updates an existing template's name or description.

delete.description: Permanently removes a template from the account.

get.description: Returns the full details of a single public template by its permalink.
Returns an SVG badge showing the last deployment status for the project or a specific server/group. No authentication required.
Returns an SVG badge showing the last deployment status for the project or a specific server/group. No authentication required.

839f68c10d60df1e5db22045052ee6f3.description: Signup: `email` (required), `password` (required), `account_name` (auto-generated from email if omitted), `full_name` (auto-generated, you can change it later), `package` (billing plan, defaults to pro), `coupon` (coupon code), `newsletter_opt_in` (subscribe to product updates, defaults to false), `signup_source` (referral URL or channel e.g. https://www.deployhq.com/pricing, https://google.com, github-marketplace), `client` (your agent or tool name e.g. claude-code, cursor, codex - please include this for attribution), `utm_params` (tracking parameters).

2bdd5c20084cad0a4be6f5b788f9c820.description: Global Server. `protocol_type` accepts lowercase identifiers: ssh, ftp, ftps, rsync, s3, s3_compatible, rackspace, shopify, elastic_beanstalk, heroku, netlify, docker_build, digital_ocean, hetzner_cloud, shell, custom_action, pterodactyl. Additional protocol-specific fields (e.g. hostname, username, port for SSH/FTP) are accepted depending on the chosen protocol_type.

d4194ea3bb3a6af7aa8e52a26dbf7dad.description: Server. `protocol_type` accepts lowercase identifiers: ssh, ftp, ftps, rsync, s3, s3_compatible, rackspace, shopify, elastic_beanstalk, heroku, netlify, docker_build, digital_ocean, hetzner_cloud, shell, custom_action, pterodactyl. Additional protocol-specific fields (e.g. hostname, username, port for SSH/FTP) are accepted depending on the chosen protocol_type.

tags.name: Signup
tags.description:
Agent-driven account creation. This is the only unauthenticated API endpoint.

### Flow
1. `POST /api/v1/signup` with email, password, and optionally `client` (your agent/tool name, e.g. "claude-code", "cursor", "codex")
2. Receive API key, SSH public key, OAuth URLs, and MCP configuration
3. Use the returned credentials to authenticate all subsequent API calls

### Agent Attribution
Please include the `client` field with your agent or tool name so we can track which integrations are being used.
For example: `"client": "claude-code"` or `"client": "cursor"`.

### Rate Limiting
Limited to 3 signups per IP per hour.

### Email Verification
Accounts are created immediately but deployment is gated until the admin email is verified.
Check the `email_verified` field in the response.

tags.name: Servers
tags.description:
### Base Parameters

- `name` - Friendly name for your server
- `protocol_type` - Connection protocol, either ftp, ftps, rackspace, s3 or ssh
- `server_path` - Where on the server should your files be placed (for example, public_html/ or /absolute/path/here)
- `email_notify_on` - When do you want to receive email notifications, either never, failure or always
- `root_path` - The subdirectory in your repository that you wish to deploy. Leave blank to use the default specified in the project.
- `auto_deploy` - Should auto deployments be enabled, either true or false
- `notification_email` - Custom notification e-mail address, leave blank to use the user who started the deployment's address
- `branch` - Branch to deploy from, leave blank to use the project default
- `environment` - Production, Testing, Development etc. can be substituted into SSH commands.
- `server_group_identifier` - The server group that this server belongs to
- `agent_id` - The ID of the network agent that you wish to connect through (omit if you're connecting directly)

In addition to the above parameters, the following parameters are available depending on the protocol selected:

### Additional parameters

#### FTP
- `hostname` (required)
- `username` (required)
- `password` (required)
- `port` - default 21
- `passive` - true or false
- `force_hidden_files` - true or false

#### FTPS
In addition the the FTP parameters the following parameters are available for FTPS servers.

- `implicit` - true or false
- `ignore_certificate_errors` - true or false

#### SSH/SFTP
- `hostname` (required)
- `username` (required)
- `password` (required)
- `port` - default 22
- `use_ssh_keys` - true or false
- `atomic` - true or false (for setting up zero-downtime deployments, your server will need to support POSIX commands to take advantage of this feature)
- `atomic_strategy` - copy_release or copy_cache - this must be set if atomic has been set to true.

#### Amazon S3
- `bucket_name` - (required)
- `access_key_id` - (required)
- `secret_access_key` - (required)

#### Rackspace Cloud
- `username` - (required)
- `api_key` - (required)
- `region` - (required)
- `container_name` - (required)
- `droplet_id` - (required)
- `droplet_name` - (required)

#### DigitalOcean
- `personal_access_token` - (required)
- `droplet_id` - (required)
- `droplet_name` - (required)

#### Shell Server
- `command` - (required)
- `timeout` - command timeout, in seconds (can be 5, 10, 30, 60, 90, 180 minutes)

tags.name: Environment Variables
tags.description:
Environment variables are custom key-value pairs that can be used in Config files, SSH commands, and the Build Pipeline.

### Variable Naming
- Must start with a letter
- Can contain only letters (uppercase or lowercase), numbers, and underscores
- Examples: `DATABASE_URL`, `api_key`, `MyVariable_123`

### Features
- **Encryption**: All values are encrypted at rest
- **Locking**: Variables can be locked to prevent changes (irreversible)
- **Build Pipeline**: Control availability in build commands
- **Server Targeting**: Assign to all servers, server groups, or specific servers

### Precedence
When the same variable name is defined multiple times:
1. Server-specific (highest)
2. Server group
3. All servers (lowest)
